name: Eddie Daniels
abstract: Eddie Daniels (born October 19, 1941) is an American musician and composer. Although he is best known as a jazz clarinetist, he has also played saxophone and flute as well as classical music on clarinet.

concerts.label: Michel Legrand, His Orchestra and Friends

about_quotes.speaker: Adrian Cunningham
about_quotes.evidence: I actually came over to the States a few times to study with Eddie Daniels, because he’s a huge influence on me
about_quotes.doc_title: Adrian Cunningham interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 31, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Daniels, Garnett Brown on trombone, myself. And we were the young musicians in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Daniels was in the band, Alex Foster, Jimmy Madison played drums and Jay Leonhart played bass. It was a really good band that he called the New York All-Star Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: Trumpets: Jon Faddis... Saxes: George Young... Eddie Daniels... We did sixteen takes in a three hour session.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: The saxophone section was Jerome Richardson and Jerry Dodgion and Joe Farrell and Eddie Daniels and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Watrous
about_quotes.evidence: Let Eddie Daniels play, let me play... in Bob Rosengarden’s band on “The Dick Cavett Show”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999
